previous_headings: What Was Done > Phase 1, Week 3: Temporal Gap-Filling Layer — COMPLETE
title: localintel SSA Expansion — Session Log
text: DHS surveys conducted every 3–13 years (median 5), producing irregularly spaced, short time series per region (median 3 observations). Unlike Eurostat’s annual data, gaps surveys represent genuine temporal uncertainty. scientifically sound gap-filling method needed respects data bounds, passes observed values exactly, provides calibrated uncertainty intervals.

previous_headings: What Was Done > Phase 1, Week 3: Temporal Gap-Filling Layer — COMPLETE
title: localintel SSA Expansion — Session Log
text: Systematic evaluation 5+ methods via leave-one-cross-validation Kenya provinces: V1 (Pure GAM): Penalized spline mgcv::gam. Problem: smooths observed points instead interpolating exactly. Nyanza 1998: observed 199, estimated 206. V2 (Natural cubic spline + GAM SE): Exact interpolation catastrophic edge overshoot due global coupling natural cubic splines. Nairobi 2022: predicted 119 vs observed 44 (170% error LOO). V3 (FMM spline + GAM SE): Final method. FMM (Forsythe-Malcolm-Moler) cubic spline uses local tangent computation — edge overshoot, exact interpolation. GAM provides uncertainty estimates .

previous_headings: What Was Done > Phase 1, Week 3: Temporal Gap-Filling Layer — COMPLETE
title: localintel SSA Expansion — Session Log
text: Point estimates: FMM cubic spline (splinefun(method = "fmm")) transformed scale. Passes exactly survey observations. Uncertainty: Penalized GAM (mgcv::gam) provides standard errors reflecting data density, combined sigma_floor calibrated prediction intervals. Transforms: Log mortality rates (>0), logit proportions (0–100%). Applied fitting ensure predictions respect natural bounds.

previous_headings: What Was Done > Phase 1, Week 3: Temporal Gap-Filling Layer — COMPLETE
title: localintel SSA Expansion — Session Log
text: Initial GAM standard errors yielded 42.9% coverage (target: 95%) — capture smoothing uncertainty, prediction uncertainty. Fix applied two steps: Added GAM residual variance (sig2) → 62.9% coverage Added sigma_floor parameter. Sweep: 0.10→71.4%, 0.15→77.1%, 0.20→82.9%, 0.25→94.3% (Kenya), 0.30→97.1%. Selected 0.25. Multi-country LOO CV (17 countries, 505 predictions): 86.7% coverage. countries well-calibrated (Kenya 94.3%, Nigeria 95.8%, Cameroon 100%), others lower (Tanzania 77.5%, Zimbabwe 76.9%). floor may increased 0.30 better generalization.

previous_headings: What Was Done > Phase 2: Cascade & Panel Assembly — COMPLETE
what: Key Design Decision: No Multi-Level Cascading Needed
title: localintel SSA Expansion — Session Log
text: DHS data collected directly Admin 1 (subnational) level via household surveys. Unlike Eurostat, requires cascading NUTS0 → NUTS1 → NUTS2 (coalescing parent-level values fill child-level gaps), DHS Admin 1 already bottom level. “cascade” DHS therefore thin wrapper : Aligns gap-filled data Admin 1 reference skeleton (every region × year) Reshapes long format (one tibble per indicator) wide format (one column per indicator) Harmonises output format match Eurostat conventions: <var>, src_<var>_level, imp_<var>_flag

title: DHS Pipeline: Sub-Saharan Africa Subnational Data
text: Since v0.3.0, localintel extends beyond EU/Eurostat cover Sub-Saharan Africa using DHS Program Indicator Data API. DHS pipeline mirrors Eurostat workflow — fetch, process, gap-fill, cascade, visualize — adapts layer DHS survey data characteristics: irregular temporal spacing (3–13 years surveys), Admin 1 geographic units, region-name instability across survey waves. pipeline covers 62 indicators across 8 thematic domains 44 SSA countries (35 subnational survey data).

title: DHS Pipeline: Sub-Saharan Africa Subnational Data
text: DHS surveys conducted every 3–13 years (median 5). gap-filling layer interpolates survey waves using two-component design: Point estimates: FMM cubic spline (passes exactly observed survey values — smoothing) Uncertainty: Penalized GAM standard errors calibrated sigma_floor parameter 95% prediction interval coverage Transforms ensure natural bounds respected: logit proportions (0–100%), log rates (>0).
code: # Single region time series result <- gapfill_series(   years     = c(2003, 2008, 2014, 2022),   values    = c(115, 74, 52, 44),   transform = "log"    # mortality: always positive ) # Returns annual estimates 2003-2022 with CI  # With ETS forecasting beyond last survey result_fcast <- gapfill_series(   years       = c(2003, 2008, 2014, 2022),   values      = c(115, 74, 52, 44),   transform   = "log",   forecast_to = 2025 ) # Adds damped-trend forecasted rows through 2025 # All regions for one indicator across multiple countries gf_u5m <- gapfill_indicator(   indicator_id = "CM_ECMR_C_U5M",   indicator_name = "u5_mortality",   country_ids = tier1_codes(),   transform   = "log",   forecast_to = 2024 ) # $gapfilled: tibble with year, estimate, ci_lo, ci_hi, source, geo, indicator # $summary: tibble with coverage stats per country # Full pipeline: all 62 indicators × all SSA countries all_gf <- gapfill_all_dhs(   country_ids = ssa_codes(),   forecast_to = 2024 ) # Returns named list: one gap-filled tibble per indicator

title: DHS Pipeline: Sub-Saharan Africa Subnational Data
text: cascade layer assembles gap-filled output wide panel aligned Admin 1 reference skeleton — DHS counterpart cascade_to_nuts2().
code: panel <- cascade_to_admin1(all_gf, include_ci = TRUE) # Columns: geo, admin0, year, u5_mortality, u5_mortality_ci_lo, ... #           src_u5_mortality_level (always 1L = Admin 1 direct), #           imp_u5_mortality_flag (0=observed, 1=interpolated, 2=forecasted)  # Balance the panel (drop thin coverage) balanced <- balance_dhs_panel(panel,   min_countries  = 5,   # indicator must cover >= 5 countries   min_indicators = 10   # region must have >= 10 non-NA indicators )

title: DHS Pipeline: Sub-Saharan Africa Subnational Data
text: DHS Eurostat pipelines produce harmonised panel formats:

title: DHS Pipeline: Sub-Saharan Africa Subnational Data
text: DHS country codes differ ISO 8 countries (e.g., BT=Botswana, BU=Burundi, MD=Madagascar). package handles mapping internally. Region name harmonization achieves 100% geometry match rate across 652 DHS regions via 6-pass system: normalized text → manual crosswalk → composite split → dissolve lookup → non-geographic strata → fuzzy matching. Gap-fill calibration: sigma_floor=0.25 parameter yields ~87% leave-one-cross-validation coverage across 17 countries (505 predictions). Set sigma_floor=0.30 conservative intervals.

title: localintel – Inequality Mapping Engine for Subnational Data
text: Fetch DHS indicators, gap-fill time series, assemble Admin-1 panel: run entire pipeline one call:
code: library(localintel)  # 1. Fetch & process DHS indicators for SSA raw  <- fetch_dhs_batch(dhs_mortality_codes(), country_ids = ssa_codes()) proc <- process_dhs_batch(raw)  # 2. Gap-fill with GAM-based interpolation gapfilled <- gapfill_all_dhs(proc)  # 3. Cascade to Admin 1 panel with national fallback panel <- cascade_to_admin1(gapfilled)  # 4. Balance the panel (drop thin indicators/regions) balanced <- balance_dhs_panel(panel, min_countries = 5)  # 5. Map a single indicator plot_dhs_map(balanced$panel, var = "u5_mortality", year = 2020) result <- dhs_pipeline(   country_ids = ssa_codes(),   indicator_codes = c(dhs_mortality_codes(), dhs_nutrition_codes()),   forecast_to = 2025 )

code: library(localintel)  # 1. Fetch data from multiple domains econ  <- fetch_eurostat_batch(economy_codes(), level = 2, years = 2015:2024) hlth  <- fetch_eurostat_batch(health_system_codes(), level = 2, years = 2015:2024) lab   <- fetch_eurostat_batch(labour_codes(), level = 2, years = 2015:2024)  # 2. Merge, cascade to NUTS2, and impute temporal gaps all_data <- merge_datasets(gdp, beds, unemp) cascaded <- cascade_to_nuts2(   all_data,   vars = c("gdp", "beds", "unemployment_rate"),   years = 2015:2024,   impute = TRUE,        # adaptive econometric imputation (PCHIP + ETS)   forecast_to = 2025    # extend series with AIC-selected forecasts )  # Check traceability table(cascaded$src_gdp_level)  # 2=NUTS2, 1=NUTS1, 0=NUTS0 table(cascaded$imp_gdp_flag)   # 0=observed, 1=interpolated, 2=forecasted  # 4. Export for Tableau sf_all <- build_multi_var_sf(   cascaded, geopolys,   vars = c("gdp", "beds", "unemployment_rate"),   years = 2015:2024,   var_labels = regional_var_labels(),   pillar_mapping = regional_domain_mapping() ) export_to_geojson(sf_all, "output/multi_domain_nuts2.geojson")

what: Cascade Data to NUTS2 (Generic / Domain-Agnostic) — cascade_to_nuts2
title: Cascade Data to NUTS2 (Generic / Domain-Agnostic) — cascade_to_nuts2
text: Cascades data NUTS0/NUTS1 NUTS2 level set variables thematic domain. Unlike cascade_to_nuts2_and_compute(), function performs pure cascading without computing domain-specific derived indicators, making suitable economy, education, labour, environment, Eurostat domain. impute = TRUE (default), adaptive econometric imputation applied cascading fill temporal gaps within region's time series. uses PCHIP interpolation internal gaps optionally ETS autoregressive forecasting future periods (forecast_to specified). best model selected automatically via AIC.

title: Cascade Data to NUTS2 (Generic / Domain-Agnostic) — cascade_to_nuts2
code: cascade_to_nuts2(   data,   vars,   years = NULL,   nuts2_ref = NULL,   nuts_year = 2024,   impute = TRUE,   forecast_to = NULL )

title: Cascade Data to NUTS2 (Generic / Domain-Agnostic) — cascade_to_nuts2
text: data Dataframe 'geo', 'year', variable columns. May contain data mixed NUTS levels (identified geo code length: 2=NUTS0, 3=NUTS1, 4=NUTS2). vars Character vector variable names cascade years Integer vector years include. NULL, uses available years. nuts2_ref Reference table get_nuts2_ref(). NULL, fetched automatically (session-cached instant repeated access). nuts_year Integer year NUTS classification (default: 2024) impute Logical. TRUE (default), apply adaptive econometric imputation (PCHIP + optional ETS) fill temporal gaps cascading. forecast_to Integer year. specified greater max(years), extend series ETS autoregressive forecasts year. used impute = TRUE.

title: Cascade Data to NUTS2 (Generic / Domain-Agnostic) — cascade_to_nuts2
text: Dataframe cascaded values NUTS2 level. variable v: src_v_level: source NUTS level (2 = original NUTS2,     1 = cascaded NUTS1, 0 = cascaded NUTS0) imp_v_flag: imputation flag (0 = observed/cascaded,     1 = PCHIP interpolated, 2 = ETS forecasted). present     impute = TRUE.

title: Cascade Data to NUTS2 (Generic / Domain-Agnostic) — cascade_to_nuts2
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Cascade with adaptive imputation (default) result <- cascade_to_nuts2(   all_data,   vars = c("gdp", "unemployment_rate"),   years = 2015:2024,   impute = TRUE )  # Cascade with imputation + forecasting to 2025 result <- cascade_to_nuts2(   all_data,   vars = c("gdp", "unemployment_rate"),   years = 2015:2024,   impute = TRUE,   forecast_to = 2025 )  # Check traceability table(result$src_gdp_level)   # cascade source table(result$imp_gdp_flag)    # imputation method  # Cascade without imputation (legacy behaviour) result <- cascade_to_nuts2(   all_data,   vars = c("gdp", "unemployment_rate"),   years = 2015:2024,   impute = FALSE ) } # }

what: PCHIP Interpolation with Constant Endpoints — interp_pchip_flag
title: PCHIP Interpolation with Constant Endpoints — interp_pchip_flag
text: Performs Piecewise Cubic Hermite Interpolating Polynomial (PCHIP) interpolation missing values within observed range. Uses monotone Hermite splines (Fritsch-Carlson method) via splinefun robust non-linear patterns avoid overshooting knots. Endpoints beyond observed data held constant (last observation carried forward/backward). function replaces previous linear interpolation approach sophisticated method preserves monotonicity handles non-linear relationships better.

what: Local Intelligence for Subnational Data Analysis — localintel-package
title: Local Intelligence for Subnational Data Analysis — localintel-package
text: comprehensive pipeline fetching, processing, visualizing subnational (NUTS 0/1/2) data Eurostat. package provides tools data fetching, cascading higher lower NUTS levels, computing composite health indicators, creating publication-ready maps, exporting data Tableau visualization tools.

text: Main Functions: Data Fetching: get_nuts2, get_nuts_level, fetch_eurostat_batch Reference Data: get_nuts2_ref, get_nuts_geopolys, get_population_nuts2 Data Cascading: cascade_to_nuts2_and_compute, cascade_to_nuts2_light Visualization: build_display_sf, plot_best_by_country_level Export: export_to_geojson, enrich_for_tableau

text: New exported utility detects renames non-standard column names returned Eurostat bulk download API (e.g. geo\TIME_PERIOD → geo, TIME_PERIOD → time). Called automatically every data entry point (get_nuts2(), get_nuts_level(), get_nuts_level_robust(), process_eurostat(), standardize_time()) downstream code can rely standard column names regardless Eurostat API version.

title: localintel 0.2.2
text: PCHIP interpolation ETS forecasting, source-level metadata (src_{var}_level) now forward/backward filled per region using tidyr::fill(.direction = "downup"). Previously, interpolated forecasted cells retained NA cascade assigned source levels observed values. “data reveals” panel now correctly formats percentage changes using proper sign symbols (+ / −) absolute values, preventing malformed strings like +-101.0% negative numbers “fell ”. EU average evolution narrative now accounts variable polarity (higherIs: 'worse' vs 'better'). decrease mortality rate correctly reported “improved”, “deteriorated”. insight narratives now use variable’s context field (e.g. “mortality outcomes”, “educational attainment”, “economic output”) instead hardcoded “health system capacity” “health outcomes”. variables lower better (e.g. mortality rate), Top 5 vs Bottom 5 differential now displayed absolute value, preventing confusing negative numbers like “-1431.1 per 100,000”.

previous_headings: Imputation Integration, Smart Caching, Tests
title: localintel 0.2.1
text: cascade_to_nuts2() now accepts impute = TRUE (default) forecast_to parameters. cascading, region’s time series automatically filled using PCHIP interpolation (internal gaps) optionally ETS forecasting (future periods, best model selected via AIC). New imp_{variable}_flag columns track imputation method per value: 0 = observed/cascaded, 1 = PCHIP interpolated, 2 = ETS forecasted. existing src_{variable}_level columns preserved cascade source tracking. Set impute = FALSE legacy behaviour (gap-filling). geometry reference functions (get_nuts_geo(), get_nuts_geopolys(), get_nuts2_ref(), get_nuts2_names()) now cache results package-level environment instant repeated access within R session. clear_localintel_cache(): Exported function reset cache force fresh data fetches. Cascade functions now use cached get_nuts2_ref() instead duplicating geospatial fetch logic. Comprehensive unit tests utils, imputation, cascade, data processing, caching functions using mock data (Eurostat API calls required).

previous_headings: Imputation Integration, Smart Caching, Tests
title: localintel 0.2.1
text: cascade_to_nuts2() cascade_to_nuts2_and_compute() now use cached get_nuts2_ref() function, avoiding redundant geospatial API calls. Added giscoR checks informative error messages functions depend geospatial data.

previous_headings: Econometric Imputation
title: localintel 0.2.0
text: interp_pchip_flag(): PCHIP (Piecewise Cubic Hermite Interpolating Polynomial) interpolation missing years within observed range. Uses monotone Hermite splines (Fritsch-Carlson method) preserve monotonicity handle non-linear patterns without overshooting — significant upgrade previous linear interpolation approach. forecast_autoregressive(): Exponential smoothing state space model (ETS) forecasting beyond observed data. Autoregressive design, automatically selects optimal smoothing parameters via AICc. Falls back Holt’s linear trend forecast package unavailable. impute_series(): Unified pipeline combining PCHIP interpolation (internal gaps) autoregressive forecasting (future periods). Returns complete series flags distinguishing observed (0), interpolated (1), forecasted (2) values.

previous_headings: Econometric Imputation
title: localintel 0.2.0
text: Interpolation vs Forecasting: package now uses two distinct methods — PCHIP within-range imputation (curve respect existing data points) ETS beyond-range forecasting (autoregressive dynamics drive projection). separation reflects fundamentally different statistical requirements task. Graceful Degradation: forecast package placed Suggests rather Imports. unavailable, system falls back Holt’s linear trend extrapolation manually estimated smoothing parameters (α=0.3, β=0.1).
